explanations.1: This disables spell-checking for this entire Dockerfile, probably because it contains URLs or technical terms that would otherwise trigger warnings.
explanations.19: We're re-declaring these ARG variables here because Docker needs to know about them again after the FROM statement, which resets the build context.
explanations.23: The double quotes around ${USERNAME} are needed because the variable might contain spaces or special characters, and without them the shell could misinterpret the command.
explanations.29: This ARG isn't actually used in the visible RUN commands below, so it might be a leftover or intended for future use.
explanations.45: This uses jq (a JSON query tool) to parse Google's Chrome version list and extract the download URL for the specific Chrome version we want, filtering for Linux 64-bit.
explanations.47: The backslash-e codes are ANSI escape sequences that make the output display in magenta; this is just for prettier console output during the build.
explanations.54: This does the same jq parsing as line 45, but this time it extracts the chromedriver (the automation tool) instead of the browser itself.
